<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build-skill.mjs — do not edit by hand.
     Sources: src/demos/, src/content/components/, src/data/features.ts -->

# Component index

One reference file per component. Read `references/<slug>.md` for the copyable
fragment(s), the explanation of the technique, and per-feature fallback behavior.

| Component | File | Description | Leading-edge features |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Accordion | `accordion.md` | Exclusive open, animated height auto, correct semantics, find-in-page support. The browser does all of it. | details-name, details-content, interpolate-size |
| Alert | `alert.md` | A callout with an icon, title and description. role="alert" does the announcing; the grid does the layout. | — |
| Alert Dialog | `alert-dialog.md` | A confirmation that interrupts on purpose — the same native <dialog> as the modal, with light dismiss switched off and alertdialog semantics. The whole diff is a few attributes. | dialog, dialog-invokers, discrete-transitions |
| Avatar | `avatar.md` | Initials-first avatars and an overlapping stack. No image? No onError handler — initials are the default, images are the enhancement. | — |
| Badge | `badge.md` | A span with utilities. Included so you can stop wondering whether the library has one. | — |
| Button | `button.md` | A plain button element with the shadcn look. No wrapper, no runtime — the platform has had this one covered since 1995. | — |
| Card | `card.md` | Header, content, footer — a bordered box with good spacing defaults, shown here wrapping real zero-JS switches. | — |
| Carousel | `carousel.md` | Scroll snap does the carousel; the CSS Carousels spec adds dots and arrow buttons the browser generates, wires up, and keeps accessible. | scroll-markers, anchor-positioning |
| Checkbox | `checkbox.md` | A native checkbox restyled with appearance-none, with an SVG check that appears via peer-checked. All the semantics, none of the ARIA. | — |
| Dialog | `dialog.md` | A modal with entry/exit animations, backdrop blur, light dismiss, focus trapping and Esc-to-close. Zero JavaScript — the button opens it declaratively. | dialog, dialog-invokers, closedby, discrete-transitions |
| Drawer | `drawer.md` | A panel that slides in from the edge — same native <dialog> as the modal, just anchored to the side and sliding instead of scaling. Focus trap, Esc and light dismiss come free. | dialog, dialog-invokers, closedby, discrete-transitions |
| Dropdown Menu | `dropdown-menu.md` | Top-layer rendering, Esc + click-outside dismiss, anchored under the trigger, auto-flips at viewport edges. All attributes and CSS. | popover, anchor-positioning, discrete-transitions |
| Header | `header.md` | A sticky app bar that hides as you scroll down and slides back when you scroll up — plus a section header that lifts when it docks. Pure CSS, driven by container scroll-state queries. | scroll-state-scrolled, scroll-state-stuck |
| Hover Card | `hover-card.md` | A rich preview card that appears when you hover or focus a link — native delays, keyboard support and viewport-aware flipping, all handed to the browser via an interest invoker. | interest-invokers, anchor-positioning, discrete-transitions |
| Input | `input.md` | A styled text input with free validation states — :user-invalid flags bad values with zero JavaScript, and only after the user actually touched the field. | user-invalid |
| Label | `label.md` | A form field row — label, input, and help text. The for attribute has been the accessibility API this whole time. | — |
| Meter | `meter.md` | Gauge semantics with automatic good/warn/critical coloring driven by the low/high/optimum attributes — the browser picks the state, CSS picks the colors. | — |
| Navigation Menu | `nav-menu.md` | A horizontal nav where a highlight pill slides between links on hover and focus — CSS anchor positioning makes a pseudo-element track whichever link owns the anchor. | anchor-inset |
| Popover | `popover.md` | A trigger and a free-form panel in the top layer — anchored under the button, light-dismissed, focus handled. Same machinery as the dropdown menu, pointed at arbitrary content. | popover, anchor-positioning, discrete-transitions |
| Progress | `progress.md` | The native progress element, restyled per engine. Screen readers announce the percentage without a single ARIA attribute. | — |
| Radio Group | `radio-group.md` | Native radios in a fieldset — the selected dot is a border-width trick, and arrow-key navigation is the platform's roving tabindex. | — |
| Scroll Area | `scroll-area.md` | A scroll container with a thin themed scrollbar and fade edges that appear only when there's more to scroll — styled scrollbars plus a scroll-state query, no JavaScript. | scrollbar-color, scroll-state-scrollable |
| Select | `select.md` | The real <select>, opted into full styling — rich HTML in options, an animated picker panel, and a mirrored selection. Falls back to the classic native select everywhere else. | base-select, discrete-transitions |
| Skeleton | `skeleton.md` | Loading placeholders with animate-pulse. Reduced-motion users get a calm static block — that's in the token paste, not extra work. | — |
| Slider | `slider.md` | input type=range with a styled track and thumb. Keyboard stepping, Home/End, form participation — all native. | — |
| Switch | `switch.md` | A checkbox in a trench coat. CSS has been able to do this since forever — no ARIA gymnastics required. | — |
| Tabs | `tabs.md` | Radio inputs wearing tab clothes. Arrow-key switching comes free — it's the native radio group behavior. | — |
| Textarea | `textarea.md` | A textarea that grows with its content. The autogrow JavaScript you've written five times is one CSS declaration now. | field-sizing |
| Tooltip | `tooltip.md` | Two tiers — a pure :hover/:focus tooltip that works everywhere, and interest-invoker toolbar tips with smart delays and viewport-aware flipping. | interest-invokers, anchor-positioning, discrete-transitions |
